Daphne L. Macklin 
American Civil Liberties Union 
Legislative Office 
1311 I street, Suite 102 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Ms. Macklin: 
April 19, 1983 
This is in response to your letter April 7, 1983, in which you 
requested advice regarding the filings of the Northern and Southern 
California affiliates of the American Civil Liberties Union as 
lobbyist employers. 
In your letter and in our previous conversation you stated that the 
two affiliates contribute equal amounts for salary (including 
salary to one or more lobbyists) and office expenses of the ACLU 
Legislative Office in Sacramento. 
You also stated that the 
Northern and Southern ACLU affiliates maintain independent 
authority and direction over the staff of the legislative office. 
Since the affiliates do not act in concert in directing the staff 
of the legislative office to influence legislative or 
administrative action, but only share the staff and facilities, 
each should file a Form 600A authorizing all lobbyists in the 
legislative office, and quarterly Lobbyist Employer Reports 
(Form 650) showing payment of one-half of the salary for lobbyists 
and support staff and office expenses. 
A notation may be made on 
the Form 650 indicating that the other affiliate is providing an 
equal amount. 
If I can be of any further assistance, please do not hesitiate to 
contact me. 
Yours Very Truly, 
Lynn Montgomery 
Political Reform Consultant 
LM:cm

sections.question_synthetic: Whether two separate ACLU affiliates that jointly fund a shared Sacramento legislative office should file as a single lobbyist employer or separately, given that they contribute equal amounts but maintain independent authority over the staff.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Each affiliate should file separately: Form 600A authorizing all lobbyists in the office and quarterly Form 650 reports showing payment of one-half of lobbyist salaries and office expenses, with notation that the other affiliate provides equal funding. The key is that they do not act in concert in directing staff.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether two separate ACLU affiliates that jointly fund a shared Sacramento legislative office should file as a single lobbyist employer or separately, given that they contribute equal amounts but maintain independent authority over the staff.

CONCLUSION: Each affiliate should file separately: Form 600A authorizing all lobbyists in the office and quarterly Form 650 reports showing payment of one-half of lobbyist salaries and office expenses, with notation that the other affiliate provides equal funding. The key is that they do not act in concert in directing staff.

Macklin American Civil Liberties Union Legislative Office 1311 I street, Suite 102 Sacramento, CA 95814 Dear Ms. Macklin: April 19, 1983 This is in response to your letter April 7, 1983, in which you requested advice regarding the filings of the Northern and Southern California affiliates of the American Civil Liberties Union as lobbyist employers. In your letter and in our previous conversation you stated that the two affiliates contribute equal amounts for salary (including salary to one or more lobbyists) and office expenses of the ACLU Legislative Office in Sacramento. You also stated that the Northern and Southern ACLU affiliates maintain independent authority and direction over the staff of the legislative office. Since the affiliates do not act in concert in directing the staff of the legislative office to influence legislative or administrative action, but only share the staff and facilities, each should file a Form 600A authorizing all lobbyists in the legislative office, and quarterly Lobbyist Employer Reports (Form 650) showing payment of one-half of the salary for lobbyists and support staff and office expenses. A notation may be made on the Form 650 indicating that the other affiliate is providing an equal amount. If I can be of any further assistance, please do not hesitiate to contact me. Yours Very Truly, Lynn Montgomery Political Reform Consultant LM:cm
embedding.summary: FPPC advises that two ACLU affiliates sharing a Sacramento legislative office should each file separately as lobbyist employers (Form 600A and Form 650), reporting one-half of shared costs, rather than filing jointly.
